---
title: "Cannot write to NFS shares"
date: 2007-05-16
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by lamalex on 2007-05-16
I have two nfs shares, neither of which I can write to. According to the many guides I have read, all my settings are correct but since it's not working something is not right.
Here is my /etc/fstab
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda1 :
UUID=016b6f9a-d55c-48a9-b83c-3f009a21ac5c / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda5 :
UUID=8e0c8e29-9e87-4c10-b4e7-480a6ddf6797 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
# Entry for /media/iso
gorstag:/opt/samba/iso /media/iso nfs rsize=8192,wsize=8192,timeo=14,intr,rw
# Entry for /media/public
gorstag:/opt/samba/public /media/public nfs rsize=8192,wsize=8192,timeo=14,intr,rw

```

My /etc/exports from the server
```

# /etc/exports: the access control list for filesystems which may be exported
#               to NFS clients.  See exports(5).
#
# Example for NFSv2 and NFSv3:
# /srv/homes       hostname1(rw,sync) hostname2(ro,sync)
#
# Example for NFSv4:
# /srv/nfs4        gss/krb5i(rw,sync,fsid=0,crossmnt)
# /srv/nfs4/homes  gss/krb5i(rw,sync)
#
/opt/samba/iso 192.168.1.1/24 (rw,no_root_squash,async)
/opt/samba/public 192.168.1.1/24 (rw,no_root_squash,async)

```

anyone have any ideas? I should also note, not even root can write, it is claimed to be "read only filesystem"

### Post by echo $USER on 2007-05-17
> **Iamalex said:**
> I have two nfs shares, neither of which I can write to. According to the many guides I have read, all my settings are correct but since it's not working something is not right.
Here is my /etc/fstab
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda1 :
UUID=016b6f9a-d55c-48a9-b83c-3f009a21ac5c / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda5 :
UUID=8e0c8e29-9e87-4c10-b4e7-480a6ddf6797 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
# Entry for /media/iso
gorstag:/opt/samba/iso /media/iso nfs rsize=8192,wsize=8192,timeo=14,intr,rw
# Entry for /media/public
gorstag:/opt/samba/public /media/public nfs rsize=8192,wsize=8192,timeo=14,intr,rw

```

My /etc/exports from the server
```

# /etc/exports: the access control list for filesystems which may be exported
#               to NFS clients.  See exports(5).
#
# Example for NFSv2 and NFSv3:
# /srv/homes       hostname1(rw,sync) hostname2(ro,sync)
#
# Example for NFSv4:
# /srv/nfs4        gss/krb5i(rw,sync,fsid=0,crossmnt)
# /srv/nfs4/homes  gss/krb5i(rw,sync)
#
/opt/samba/iso 192.168.1.1/24 (rw,no_root_squash,async)
/opt/samba/public 192.168.1.1/24 (rw,no_root_squash,async)

```

anyone have any ideas? I should also note, not even root can write, it is claimed to be "read only filesystem"

First thing is to check the permissions of the shared folders in /opt/samba and make sure that is cool.  Start off by changing it to 777 then scaling back once you know it works to determine level of permissions that works best.

Also try this in exports to test if its working then implement permissions the way you want...

/opt/samba/iso                                                  192.168.1.1/24(rw,async)
/opt/samba/public                                              192.168.1.1/24(rw,async)

Here is mine for an example...

/etc/fstab
192.168.0.111:/home/public       /mnt/backup      nfs      rsize=8192,wsize=8192,timeo=14,intr

/etc/exports
/home/public                            192.168.0.177(rw,async)

And do not forget to restart NFS after making your changes...

sudo /etc/init.d/nfs-kernel-server restart

---
